run_context: 2026-05-14 scheduled run: EXP-367/368/369/329 integration + WIN-048 curmudgeon patches

patches.find: The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets.
patches.replace: The legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b requires that the data be "not plausibly known to the author at write-time," which is not the case for indexed public datasets. Verdict boundary note: WIN-006 uses “Std Model Explains” rather than “Misleading” (WIN-007, WIN-022) because the pre-1990 linear drift is accurately described at face value — both models fit the same linear trend, so there is no false causal claim, only an absence of discriminating power. WIN-007 and WIN-022 are “Misleading” because they introduce specific causal framings (“aetheric threshold crossing,” “phase transition”) for the post-1990 acceleration that are category errors: the dome asserts a mechanistic explanation it cannot derive, not merely a shared observation.
patches.rationale: Add verdict-boundary note explaining WIN-006 Std-Model-Explains vs WIN-007/022 Misleading distinction (EXP-367)

patches.find: The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity.
patches.replace: The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity. Three bright southern stars — Canopus (α Car, V=−0.74, F0Ib supergiant, ~13,600 L☉, 310 ly), Achernar (α Eri, V=0.46, B6Vep, ~3,150 L☉, 139 ly), and Acrux (α Cru, V=0.77, B0.5IV+B1V, ~25,000 L☉, 321 ly) — are all intrinsically far more luminous than Sigma Octantis, immediately falsifying any dome claim that the south polar region systematically suppresses stellar brightness: these stars are brighter, not dimmer, than dome geometry would predict if radial aetheric depth caused extinction. Earth’s axial precession (~25,772-year cycle, driven by luni-solar torques) is moving the south celestial pole toward Canopus; in approximately 6,000 years Canopus will serve as a far brighter south pole star than Sigma Octantis is today — demonstrating that the apparent dimness near the south pole is a transient coincidence of current-epoch stellar geometry, not a structural feature required by dome geometry.
patches.rationale: Add Canopus/Achernar/Acrux brightness falsification + precession sentence (EXP-367 P6, ISS-1914+1915)

patches.find: Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories.
patches.replace: Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories. Note: this ΔB estimate derives from a global ensemble (5–15 nT range across 207 measurements); specific 2009 Chinese-station ΔB readings from Hangzhou, Changchun, or Beijing magnetometers would provide a more direct citation for the 2009 event but have not been independently located in English-language literature.
patches.rationale: Add epistemic qualifier: ΔB estimate is from global ensemble, not direct 2009 Chinese-station citation (EXP-368)

patches.find: the dome's V13 regresses when asked to cover a broader surface range. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is in-sample retrodiction, not prediction.
patches.replace: the dome's V13 regresses when asked to cover a broader surface range. A defender may also invoke information-criterion arguments — AIC and BIC penalize complexity, so the four-parameter V13 is supposedly justified if its residual improvement outweighs the penalty. We grant the principle: AIC/BIC are the standard apparatus for nested-model comparison. But the framework collapses here on its own terms. The small-sample correction (AICc) at n = 6, k = 4 adds 2k(k + 1)/(n − k − 1) = 40 penalty units — an arithmetic signal that the n/k ratio is below the regime in which information-criterion selection is reliable at all. Beyond the small-sample failure, AIC/BIC penalties are derived asymptotically: they quantify expected loss when the chosen model is applied to new data drawn from the same distribution. When the model excludes a slice of its prospective domain by stipulation (OPEN-015, cities within ±12°), that out-of-sample regime never arrives, and the asymptotic justification for the complexity penalty has no purchase. Most fundamentally, information criteria adjudicate among candidate models; they cannot rescue any candidate from being absolutely worse than a non-nested competitor. WGS84 reaches 0.01% RMSE globally with two parameters; V13 reaches 6.2% on its six fit routes with four. No information-criterion argument bridges that 600× gap. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is in-sample retrodiction, not prediction.

patches.find: The robust first harmonic at ~14.3 Hz directly contradicts any resonance ceiling below that frequency. See WIN-008 for full analysis.
patches.replace: The robust first harmonic at ~14.3 Hz directly contradicts any resonance ceiling below that frequency. Measured Schumann harmonics span 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz; all four harmonics above the fundamental fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula, and none corresponds to 11.7 Hz. See WIN-008 for full analysis.
patches.rationale: Add quantitative Schumann harmonic list for standalone readability (EXP-369 P0)

patches.find: Vindication note: the σ kept growing exactly as the Fisher arithmetic predicts
patches.replace: Mechanical-prediction check: the σ kept growing exactly as the Fisher arithmetic predicts

patches.replace: As of this review, none of the {{TOTAL_WINS}} claims demonstrate predictive power exceeding mainstream geophysical models.

patches.find: Net effect on verdict: "Misleading" still applies. The weakened claim is less misleading than the original, and we credit the author for the correction, but the structural problems (false dilemma, self-contradiction with WIN-034, no dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry) are independent of the σ-value and remain fatal.
patches.replace: Net effect on verdict: the WIN is Self-Contradicted (escalated from Misleading 2026-05-09 per EXP-295). The weakened statistical claim is less misleading than the original and we credit the author for the correction, but the structural problems — copper-firmament opacity to the 160 GHz CMB (WIN-034 self-contradiction), false-dilemma framing of ΛCDM anomalies as dome evidence, and no dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry — operate at a different level from the σ-value. Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper or the absence of a dome cosmology, so the structural verdict stands independent of how the Planck reanalysis lands.
patches.rationale: Fix stale detail_extra closing para: 'Misleading still applies' contradicts current Self-Contradicted verdict (WIN-048.c5 major hole)

patches.find:
— the firmament cannot be metaphorical for one purpose and physical for another.

The dome model provides no mechanism
patches.replace:
— the firmament cannot be metaphorical for one purpose and physical for another. Our review classifies WIN-034 itself as Unfalsifiable on direct material-sampling grounds (we cannot sample the alleged dome material). That classification does not exempt a published dome commitment from load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning — the dome publishes cast-copper firmament as a physical specification and invokes it as Schumann conductive ceiling; we are asking whether the dome can coherently hold both that commitment and the CMB-observability premise of WIN-048.

The dome model provides no mechanism
patches.rationale: Pre-rebut dome defender's 'can't use Unfalsifiable claim as falsifying premise' argument (WIN-048.c5 moderate hole, c4 option-a carry-over)
